<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>**Updating server status**

I made some edits to the code and ran the development environment. There was a PowerShell cat issue, but the output indicated that Port 5000 was in use, so I switched to Port 5001, and now the server is running successfully on that port. 

I'll provide a concise status update, emphasizing that the server URL changes depending on the port. I’ll suggest using the PORT environment variable. Keeping it minimal is the goal!</details></think>

---

I updated the server to auto-increment the port when it’s busy and verified it started on 5001.

- You can now start the app normally; if 5000 is taken, it will try the next ports until one works.
- Optional: set a base port via environment variable `PORT` (e.g., `set PORT=4000 && npm run dev` on Windows).

- Open: http://localhost:5001/ (or whichever port it logs)

- Changes: `index.js` now retries on `EADDRINUSE` and logs the chosen port.

---
